full_prompt: Design the roll bar (primary rollover structure) for a student-built ultra-efficient urban-concept vehicle, compliant with a governing structural-demonstration rulebook (2026 edition). The driver sits upright; the roll bar must exceed the driver's helmet top by at least 50 mm with helmet on. Vehicle kerb mass including driver = 200 kg; the roll bar is anchored to the chassis at two rear-mount points and two forward-mount points (4-point mount). Material: 4130 chromoly steel tubing, primary members 25.4 mm (1 in) OD x 2.0 mm wall. E = 205 GPa, yield = 460 MPa, ultimate = 670 MPa, density = 7850 kg/m^3. Geometric constraints: roll bar height above floor 1200 mm (clearing a 1150 mm helmet + driver sitting height); width 700 mm outside-outside; depth (fore-aft at top) 400 mm. Mounted to chassis via four M10 Grade 8.8 bolts. Load cases from the governing rollover-structure article: (R1) Vertical load: 700 N applied at any point on the upper surface of the roll bar at any angle within 15 deg of vertical; max von Mises in tube <= yield / 1.15 = 400 MPa; deflection at application point <= 5 cm (50 mm) to maintain helmet clearance. (R2) Lateral load: 300 N applied horizontally at any point on the upper surface; max deflection <= 25 mm; max von Mises <= 400 MPa. (R3) Longitudinal load: 300 N applied horizontally from the front; same limits as R2. (R4) Combined: 700 N vertical simultaneous with 300 N lateral; max von Mises <= ultimate / 1.5 = 447 MPa. (R5) Roll bar mass <= 3.0 kg. (R6) Bulkhead / fuel-cell separation: the roll bar shall not intrude into the fuel-cell compartment (if H2-powered) under any case; this is a no-intrusion geometric check rather than a stress check. Primary verification: structural FEA (linear static, 4 cases). Vibration, thermal, fluid, radiation, buckling NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 welded roll bar, weld map, and a report listing mass in kg, max deflection per case in mm, max von Mises per case in MPa.
short_prompt:
Design the primary rollover structure (roll bar) for a student-built ultra-efficient urban-concept vehicle, compliant with the governing structural-demonstration rulebook (2026 edition). Driver sits upright; the roll bar must clear the helmet top by >= 50 mm with helmet on. Kerb mass with driver = 200 kg. Primary members are 25.4 mm OD x 2.0 mm wall 4130 chromoly tubing, anchored to the chassis at 4 bolts (M10 Grade 8.8).

Envelope: roll-bar height above floor 1200 mm, outside-outside width 700 mm, fore-aft top depth 400 mm.

Load cases (from the rulebook's rollover-structure article).
- LC1 vertical 700 N at any point on the upper surface within 15 deg of vertical; load-point deflection <= 50 mm.
- LC2 lateral 300 N horizontal at any point on the upper surface; deflection <= 25 mm.
- LC3 longitudinal 300 N horizontal from the front; deflection <= 25 mm.
- LC4 combined: 700 N vertical + 300 N lateral simultaneous (ultimate-load case).
- LC5 no-intrusion geometric check into the fuel-cell compartment (if H2-powered) under any case.

prompt.title: Ultra-efficient urban-concept vehicle roll bar
prompt.brief: 4130 roll bar with helmet clearance >=50 mm; 700 N vertical + 300 N lat/long load cases per the governing rollover-structure article.

boundary_conditions.mounting: four M10 bolt holes fully fixed
boundary_conditions.loads: point loads at tube apex per the governing rollover-structure article

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Quasi-static per the governing rollover-structure article.
